join_order.notes: Load sub/fy.bin as flat int16_t[86135] array (172KB, L1-resident). Also build fy2023_bits: DenseBitmap(86135) where fy==2023. Empirically: 26612 qualifying adsh_codes (30.9% of sub).

join_order.build: pre(filtered: stmt='IS' AND adsh in fy2023_set)
join_order.notes: Sampling confirmed: IS+fy2023 rows = 554253 (31.4% of IS rows). is_map capacity ~1M entries x 16 bytes = 16MB — fits in L3 (44MB). Order B is 4.31x faster than Order A (23.4s vs 5.4s single-thread). No next_is linked-list array: use forward scan in pre for the 0.7% of keys with duplicate (adsh,tagver) entries.

filters.predicate: stmt_code == is_code AND adsh_code in fy2023_bitmap
filters.notes: Applied during pre scan to build smaller is_map (5.8% of pre rows pass)

data_structures.sub_fy_array: int16_t[86135] direct array (172KB, L1-resident) — indexed by adsh_code
data_structures.fy2023_bitmap: DenseBitmap(86135) — 10.8KB, L1-resident; marks adsh_codes where sub_fy==2023
data_structures.is_map: CompactHashMap<uint64_t,uint32_t>(expected=550374) — ~16MB, L3-resident; key=(adsh_code<<32|tagver_code), value=first_pre_row in pre for this IS+fy2023 key
data_structures.thread_local_agg: CompactHashMap<uint64_t,AggEntry>(expected=16384) per thread — key=(adsh_code<<32|pre_row), value={double sum_value, int64_t cnt}
data_structures.final_agg: std::unordered_map<std::string,FinalEntry> reserved for ~600000 groups; key=name+'\x01'+tag_string+'\x01'+plabel — CORRECTNESS FIX: use decoded tag STRING, not raw tagver_code bytes

parallelism.build_phase: PARALLEL OMP: 64 threads each scan a range of pre rows, collect IS+fy2023 (key,pre_row) pairs into per-thread vectors; then serial merge builds global is_map — eliminates single-threaded bottleneck
parallelism.scan_phase: PARALLEL OMP: morsel-driven over 395 num blocks (100K rows each), atomic next_block counter

build_phase_detail.step1: Load sub/fy.bin (172KB). Build int16_t sub_fy[86135]. Build DenseBitmap fy2023_bits(86135): set bit for each adsh_code where sub_fy[adsh]==2023.
build_phase_detail.step2_parallel_collect: OMP parallel loop over pre rows [0..pre_N). Each thread scans its range. Per row i: if pre_stmt[i]==is_code AND fy2023_bits.test(pre_adsh[i]) → emit pair (key=(adsh_code<<32|tagver_code), pre_row=i) to thread_local vector. Each thread's vector is in row-ascending order.
build_phase_detail.step3_serial_merge: Single-threaded: iterate all per-thread vectors in thread-index order (thread 0 first, preserving row order). For each (key, pre_row): if key NOT in is_map → is_map.insert(key, pre_row). If key already present → skip (we keep the first/smallest pre_row). This correctly captures the first IS+fy2023 pre_row for each (adsh,tagver) key.
build_phase_detail.duplicate_handling: For the ~0.7% of is_map keys with multiple IS+fy2023 pre rows (3879 duplicate keys): during main scan, after finding first_pre_row via is_map, linearly scan forward in pre (pre_adsh[r]==adsh AND pre_tagver[r]==tagver AND pre_stmt[r]==is_code) to process all additional pre rows for that key. Since pre is sorted by (adsh_code, tagver_code), duplicates are always contiguous. Eliminates the 38.4MB next_is array entirely.

indexes.layout: uint32_t n_blocks; then n_blocks x ZoneMap{int8_t min_uom, int8_t max_uom, [2-byte pad], int32_t min_ddate, int32_t max_ddate} = 12 bytes each

aggregation_detail.phase2_key: CORRECTNESS FIX — composite string: name_sv + '\x01' + TAG_STRING + '\x01' + plabel_sv where TAG_STRING is decoded from tagver_code via tag_data[tag_offsets[tagver_code]..tag_offsets[tagver_code+1]]. DO NOT use raw tagver_code bytes as tag proxy — this was the iter_0 bug. Different tagver_codes can decode to the same tag string (same tag, different version); they must be merged into the same group.
phase2_accumulators: int32_t tagver_code (first seen, for tag decode in output)

decode: name: sub_name_data[sub_name_offsets[adsh_code]..sub_name_offsets[adsh_code+1]] — extract from phase2 composite key (before first '\x01')

tag: tag_data[tag_offsets[tagver_code]..tag_offsets[tagver_code+1]] — extract from phase2 composite key (between first and second '\x01'... BUT tag is already in the key as a string — extract from key directly by parsing between delimiters)
output.topk_strategy: partial_sort or nth_element+sort on phase2 groups

optimization_notes: Two bugs fixed in this iteration. Correctness: iter_0 used raw tagver_code bytes as the GROUP BY tag proxy in the final string re-aggregation. Since tagver_code encodes (tag, version) while SQL groups by n.tag (tag string only), rows with the same tag but different versions were split into separate groups instead of merged — causing ~15% undercount in cnt and total_value. Fix: decode the actual tag string from tag_data[tag_offsets[tagver_code]..] and embed it in the composite group key. Performance: iter_0 built is_map from ALL 1.75M IS pre rows (capacity 4M x 16B = 64MB, exceeding L3=44MB) in a single-threaded loop — sampling showed 23.4s for Order A. Applying Category A/B fix (Order B, empirically validated): pre-filter pre rows by both stmt='IS' AND adsh_code in fy2023 DenseBitmap, reducing is_map to 550K entries (16MB, L3-resident). Sampling confirms 4.31x speedup (23.4s to 5.4s single-threaded). Additional fix: parallelize the pre scan using 64 OMP threads collecting IS+fy2023 pairs into per-thread vectors, then serial merge — eliminates the single-threaded build bottleneck. The 38.4MB next_is linked-list array is replaced by a forward scan in pre for the rare 0.7% of keys with duplicate (adsh,tagver) entries (leveraging pre's sorted order).
